2606.09677.title: MeCo: One-Step MeanFlow-based Corrector for Multi-Channel Speech Separation
2606.09677.paper_summary: While discriminative models for multi-channel speech separation excel in reference-based metrics, they often exhibit suboptimal human listening quality. To address this, we propose a novel MeanFlow-based one-step generative corrector (MeCo). MeCo learns a conditional average velocity field to map discriminative estimates directly onto the clean speech manifold in a single step. To maximize one-step generation performance, we introduce Data-Space Optimization (DSO). DSO integrates an $\mathbf{x}_r$-loss, which penalizes prediction errors on longer displacement intervals to serve as a generative objective for human listening quality, with an Endpoint SI-SDR loss that directly optimizes terminal signal fidelity. Experiments demonstrate that MeCo achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance with minimal computational overhead, simultaneously achieving superior signal fidelity and human listening quality in both in-domain and out-of-domain scenarios.
2606.09677.paper_summary_zh: 雖然多通道語音分離的判別模型在基於參考的指標上表現優異，但它們往往顯示出次優的人類聆聽質量。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種新穎的基於MeanFlow的一步生成校正器（MeCo）。MeCo學習一個條件平均速度場，將判別估計直接映射到乾淨語音流形上，僅需一步。為了最大化一步生成性能，我們引入了數據空間優化（DSO）。DSO整合了一個$\mathbf{x}_r$損失，該損失對較長位移區間的預測誤差進行懲罰，以作為人類聆聽質量的生成目標，並結合一個端點SI-SDR損失，該損失直接優化終端信號的保真度。實驗表明，MeCo在計算開銷最小的情況下達到了最先進的（SOTA）性能，同時在領域內和領域外場景中實現了優越的信號保真度和人類聆聽質量。
2606.09677.author: Dohwan Kim et.al.
